notes: The History major covers a vast range array of periods and geographic contexts, from the ancient Mediterranean to twenty-first century Los Angeles, early modern China to the modern Caribbean, colonial America to the Middle East. The courses delve into a wide variety of themes and issues, including global and regional history; the study of gender and sexuality; science and medicine; music and visual culture; diaspora and ethnicity; class and culture; war, violence, and peace. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of the spring before transfer: Two semester or three quarter courses of Western Civilization or World History One semester or two quarter courses from histories of: United States Europe Africa Asia Latin America Near East Science and technology Completion of at least three courses is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED . All courses must be completed with letters grades. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https:// history.ucla.edu and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
